name: Additive QT prolongation
actions: Avoid combining QT-prolonging drugs when possible.
If unavoidable, obtain baseline and follow-up ECG and correct electrolytes (K, Mg) (educational).
Review for additional QT-prolonging agents and other risk factors (bradycardia, structural heart disease) (educational).

rationale: Both drugs can prolong the QT interval.
Combined QT-prolonging effects can increase torsades de pointes risk.

explanation_template: {A_name} and {B_name} both can prolong the QT interval. Combined use may increase risk of significant QT prolongation and torsades de pointes, especially with electrolyte abnormalities or other QT-prolonging drugs (educational).
